briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 19, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and International Warnings
sections.content: Globally, the impacts of climate change are intensifying, and warnings about it are growing louder. According to NASA data, San Francisco is rapidly subsiding due to sea-level rise [113], and the UK experienced its wettest winter on record, reminding us of the harsh reality of climate change [119]. Furthermore, research has shown that climate change has intensified global heatwave exposure [123], and a Lancet study warned that millions could face insufficient physical activity by 2050 due to climate change [126]. Of particular concern are reports that the Florida state legislature has again blocked climate change measures [114], and Japan's investment in the U.S. is concentrated in fossil fuel-related projects, equivalent to 20% of Japan's annual greenhouse gas emissions, thereby reversing decarbonization efforts [112].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: The Republic of Korea is making diverse efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society. Gumi City in Gyeongsangbuk-do has officially begun constructing the representative model for Korea's first carbon-neutral industrial complex through a national project worth 130.2 billion KRW [17, 18, 19, 28, 38, 41, 50, 58], and in Ulsan, the National Ulsan Carbon Neutrality Science Center, which will serve as a carbon neutrality experience hub, has broken ground with completion targeted for late 2027 [21, 37, 47, 49, 60, 61, 64, 67, 71]. In the corporate sector, Hanwha Corporation has been recognized for its climate change response efforts, being selected as a CDP Climate Change Response Excellent Company for three consecutive years, and Continental Tire achieved an 'A-' rating [4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 12]. Gwacheon City and the Seoul Metropolitan Office of Education are each establishing carbon neutrality roadmaps through the development of local energy plans and the introduction of a climate budget system, respectively [23, 24, 25, 34, 36, 53]. Efforts to encourage citizen participation are also active, with citizen-led carbon neutrality practices spreading across the country, such as the expansion of 'Carbon Up-Shoe' membership in Dangjin City and the inauguration ceremony for 'Green Leaders' in Gwangju City [32, 33, 35, 52, 66, 69].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Infrastructure Development
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is showing significant progress both domestically and internationally, but at the same time, challenges that need to be addressed are also emerging. Siheung City has secured 1.3 billion KRW in national funding to expand renewable energy supply, and in particular, the 'Steelland Solar Power Project' utilizing industrial complex rooftops is drawing attention as an excellent example of achieving RE100 [76, 78, 79, 84, 87, 95, 96, 98, 106]. Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) has initiated the 'West Coast HVDC Energy Highway' construction project to stably supply abundant renewable energy from the west coast to the Seoul metropolitan area, thereby formalizing its renewable energy transmission network [100, 110]. In the political sphere, the ruling party has decided to fast-track the 'Special Act on Renewable Energy Self-Sufficient Cities' to support 'RE100 industrial complexes,' thus strengthening policy support [89, 91, 104]. However, in the Jeonbuk region, despite an abundance of renewable energy generation, power plants are idling and unable to sell the electricity produced due to transmission network issues [93], clearly demonstrating the urgent need for infrastructure expansion.

sections.title: 🌍 International Cooperation and Research for Climate Change Response
sections.content: The international community continues various collaborations and research to assess the impacts of climate change and explore response measures. At the 30th United Nations Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP30), the African Development Bank (AfDB) and development partners urged for large-scale new funding to accelerate the Great Green Wall Initiative in Africa [122]. The 2025 Health and Climate Change Country Profiles for Saint Kitts and Nevis and Haiti were released, shedding light on the situations of countries vulnerable to climate change [115, 120], and Maryland has embarked on a study to quantify the costs of climate change [127]. Meanwhile, a domestic study that reconstructed climate change over 3 million years revealed that the ocean's influence was greater than that of greenhouse gases, contributing to understanding the complexity of past climate changes [2, 13]. Research indicating that wild plants can rapidly evolve to survive climate change provided new insights into nature's adaptability [124].

briefing.closing: Today's climate briefing has highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with diverse response efforts both domestically and internationally, and the challenges that still need to be addressed. This is a time when continuous attention and action become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
